summary: Solid set with standout Peggy‑O and energetic second half
blurb: The Tulsa show delivers a tight first set highlighted by a bright Peggy‑O, then rolls into a powerful Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World sequence. Audience tape captures the live vibe despite noticeable hiss.
review:
February 6, 1979 at the Tulsa Fairgrounds Pavilion finds the Dead in fine form. The first set swings briskly through classics—Jack Straw, Loser and a lively Peggy‑O that reviewers single out as a highlight. A seamless segue from China Cat Sunflower into I Know You Rider keeps the momentum going before the band launches into a strong second half. The Estimated Prophet into Eyes of the World pairing showcases tight interplay, followed by the customary drum duet and a rousing Not Fade Away leading into Black Peter and Around & Around. An audience recording by La Fortune carries a fair amount of tape hiss and crowd chatter, but it also preserves the concert’s palpable energy, making this the go‑to source for fans wanting to experience Tulsa’s upbeat vibe.

While the sound isn’t pristine, the performance itself is solid throughout—no major slips are noted, and the setlist flow feels purposeful. For Deadheads looking to hear a respectable early‑’79 show with a memorable Peggy‑O and an engaging mid‑set jam, this recording offers a worthwhile listen.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All available analysis are positive, noting a solid performance and standout Peggy‑O; no negative comments were recorded.

best_recording.reason: Only available source; captures the full show and Tulsa’s live atmosphere despite fair‑quality tape hiss.

key_highlights: Peggy‑O shines as a set highlight with bright vocal delivery.
Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World sequence delivers tight, energetic interplay.
First set maintains solid momentum; seamless China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider transition.
Audience tape preserves the concert’s vibe even though it has noticeable hiss.
